mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower future cash flows and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns remain positive or neutral when UNRATE accelerates by >0.5pp over a rolling 3-month window across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and employment growth indicates robust aggregate demand and consumer confidence, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising HOUST and PAYEMS fails to predict positive SPX 20d returns in >50% of observed instances over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains elevated above 3.5%, the Federal Reserve is constrained from cutting rates, keeping real borrowing costs high and suppressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5% and FEDFUNDS remains above the neutral rate over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which boosts corporate profits and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns show no positive correlation with month-over-month changes in INDPRO over a 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in retail and food services sales indicates weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate revenues and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns do not turn negative when RSAFS contracts by >5% over a rolling 3-month period across multiple quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Strong employment growth combined with persistent inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive monetary policy, creating a wage-price spiral that increases input costs and compresses equity margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of rising PAYEMS and high CPIAUCSL does not predict negative SPX 60d returns in >60% of instances over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth exceeds 2.5%, the economy is in a healthy expansion phase that supports corporate earnings growth, and housing starts act as a leading indicator of sustained consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are not positive when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% and HOUST remains above its 12-month moving average over a 2-year period.
